title: detectImportOptions
summary: Inspect text or spreadsheet data and create import options for tabular readers.

gpu_support.notes: Runs on the host. The returned options are metadata consumed by later import calls.

description: `opts = detectImportOptions(filename)` samples a delimited text or spreadsheet file and returns an editable scalar struct accepted by `readtable` and, for numeric text imports, `readmatrix`.
behaviors: Detects text delimiters from comma, tab, semicolon, pipe, and whitespace-separated records unless `Delimiter` is supplied.
Infers variable names from a non-data header row and sets `NumHeaderLines` so readers import only data rows.
Infers `VariableTypes` as `double`, `logical`, `datetime`, or text (`string`/`char`) using the same token rules as `readtable`.
For spreadsheet files, uses the same sheet/range reader as `readtable` and emits spreadsheet-compatible option fields.
The returned struct intentionally contains fields understood by current RunMat readers; unsupported MATLAB option fields are not emitted.

examples.description: Detect And Reuse CSV Import Options

faqs.question: Is the returned value a MATLAB import-options object?
faqs.answer: RunMat represents import options as a scalar struct so field assignment and reader consumption work naturally in the current runtime.

faqs.question: Can I use the same options with `readmatrix`?
faqs.answer: Yes for numeric text imports. `readmatrix` consumes fields such as `Delimiter`, `NumHeaderLines`, `Range`, and `TreatAsMissing` and ignores table-specific fields.

faqs.question: Does detection read the whole file?
faqs.answer: RunMat currently reads the file into host memory and samples the parsed rows, matching the existing `readtable` import pipeline.

gpu_residency: `detectImportOptions` performs file inspection on the host and returns host metadata. It does not create GPU-resident arrays.
gpu_behavior: No acceleration provider hooks are used.
